questions.question: What is a container in the context of application delivery?

options.option: A lightweight, portable runtime that packages an app and its dependencies
options.comment: Containers bundle code and dependencies for consistent runs across environments.

options.option: A full virtual machine with its own kernel
A physical server in a datacenter
An IDE used for building applications

questions.question: How do containers differ from virtual machines (VMs)?

options.option: Containers share the host OS kernel; VMs include a guest OS
options.comment: Containers are more lightweight because they reuse the host OS kernel.

options.option: Containers always use more resources than VMs
VMs cannot run multiple apps
Containers cannot be networked

questions.question: What is a Docker image layer?

options.option: A filesystem delta representing a change in the image
options.comment: Layers form the image build history and enable caching and reuse.

options.option: A networking construct in Docker Swarm
A special container runtime
A hardware partition on the host

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Docker registry?

options.option: Store and distribute Docker images to users and CI systems
options.comment: Registries host images for pull/push workflows across teams and systems.

options.option: Manage network traffic between containers
Store database backups
Run container orchestration

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Dockerfile?

options.option: Define steps to build a Docker image reproducibly
options.comment: Dockerfiles describe image build instructions and metadata.

options.option: List network rules for containers
Configure runtime security policies
Monitor container resource usage

questions.question: What is the benefit of a multi-stage Dockerfile build?

options.option: Produce smaller final images by removing build-time artifacts
options.comment: Multi-stage builds keep only runtime artifacts in the final image.

options.option: Enable containers to run on multiple hosts simultaneously
Automatically scale containers horizontally
Provide faster network throughput

questions.question: Which Docker network mode isolates containers with their own stack?

options.option: Bridge network mode for isolated container networks
options.comment: Bridge mode creates an isolated network namespace for containers on a host.

options.option: Host mode isolates containers fully from the host
Overlay mode stores data persistently
None mode enables global networking by default

questions.question: What's a key difference between a Docker volume and a bind mount?

options.option: Volumes are managed by Docker; bind mounts map host filesystem paths
options.comment: Volumes provide portability; bind mounts link to specific host paths.

options.option: Bind mounts are encrypted by default
Volumes cannot persist data
Bind mounts only work on Windows hosts

questions.question: What is Docker Compose used for?

options.option: Define and run multi-container applications via a YAML file
options.comment: Compose simplifies running sets of services locally and in CI.

options.option: Compile Docker images from source code
Act as a production-grade orchestrator replacing Kubernetes
Provide built-in image scanning

questions.question: How does Docker Compose express service dependencies?

options.option: Using the 'depends_on' field to order service startup
options.comment: 'depends_on' controls start order but not health-based readiness by default.

options.option: Using 'volumes' to network services
Using Dockerfile ARG instructions
By setting container CPU shares only

questions.question: Which practice improves container image security?

options.option: Use minimal base images and remove unnecessary packages
options.comment: Smaller images reduce attack surface and fewer vulnerable packages.

options.option: Run all containers as root for simplicity
Disable image signing to speed up deployment
Share host /var/run/docker.sock with all containers

questions.question: What's a recommended way to handle secrets for containers?

options.option: Store secrets in a dedicated secrets manager, not in images
options.comment: Secrets managers provide secure storage and rotation for credentials.

options.option: Bake secrets into Docker images for convenience
Store secrets in plain text environment variables in images
Commit secrets to source control for backups
